raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California Assembly Bill 2587 provides a two-year window for filing any sexual assault claim, regardless of when the assault occurred.",
    "The #MeToo movement has empowered many survivors of sexual assault to share their stories to stop their attackers from committing further violence.",
    "Holding perpetrators accountable acts as a deterrent to those who may prey on others.",
    "AB 2587 aims to ensure that both individual perpetrators and entities that cover up assaults are held accountable for the harm inflicted on survivors.",
    "Survivors of sexual assault often face victim-blaming, shaming, and retaliation when they report the crime.",
    "The passage of AB 2587 will clarify the intention of previous legislation (AB 2777) regarding the accountability of perpetrators and responsible entities.",
    "AB 2587 aligns California law with current scientific findings regarding the impact and long-term effect of trauma."
  ]
}
```
